title: Grammitis whartoniana
taxonomy: Grammitis whartoniana is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of epiphytic fern that is native to the tropical regions of Asia. Grammitis whartoniana is commonly found in the wild, where it grows on the trunks and branches of trees. The classification of Grammitis whartoniana is further divided into the genus Grammitis and the family Polypodiaceae, which is a part of the larger division Pteridophyta. Grammitis whartoniana is also sometimes referred to as a member of the subfamily Grammitidinae.

It is traditionally used in some cultures for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. intro: Grammitis whartoniana is a species of fern commonly known as Wharton's Grammitis, a type of epiphytic fern that grows in tropical regions of Asia and Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of arthritis, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
